entries.speaker: edren
lines: No.

entries.speaker: edren
lines:
There has to be another way. Maren — your research,
the Archive, the cycles. There has to be a precedent where the
door closed without—

entries.speaker: maren
lines: There isn't.

entries.speaker: maren
lines:
Every cycle, someone carries the Pallor through the
door. Every cycle, they stay. That's how it closes. There is no
alternative in eleven thousand years of record.

entries.speaker: edren
lines: Then we find a twelfth way.

entries.speaker: torren
lines: Edren.

entries.speaker: torren
lines: He's already decided.

entries.speaker: lira
lines:
In the workshop. The night before. You asked me what
the world looks like after. You already knew you wouldn't see it.

entries.speaker: cael
lines:
I wanted to hear you describe it. So I could carry
that with me. Through the door.

entries.speaker: lira
lines:
You didn't come to the workshop. The night before.
You couldn't face me.

entries.speaker: cael
lines:
I couldn't face you and still do what I had to do.
You would have seen it in my eyes and you would have stopped me.

entries.speaker: lira
lines:
Edren — he didn't know either. Did he? The training
grounds. "Same time tomorrow."

entries.speaker: cael
lines:
I almost told him. The words were right there. But if
I had said them, he would have locked me in the vault himself.

entries.speaker: lira
lines:
Maren knew. She didn't know what, but she knew
something was wrong.

entries.speaker: cael
lines:
Maren always knows. That's her burden. Knowing things
and not being able to stop them.

entries.speaker: lira
lines: And you didn't tell me.

entries.speaker: cael
lines:
If I had told you, you would have found a way to
stop me. And then the door stays open.

entries.speaker: lira
lines: Edren.

entries.speaker: lira
lines: Let him go.

entries.speaker: lira
lines:
I know what this costs. I know exactly what this
costs. And I'm telling you — let him go. Because the alternative
is that the door stays open, and everything he went through was
for nothing.

entries.speaker: edren
lines:
I should have seen it. All of it. The nightmares,
the isolation. I should have—

entries.speaker: cael
lines:
You couldn't have. That was the point. The Pallor
doesn't break you where people can see it. It breaks you where
you're alone.

entries.speaker: cael
lines: This isn't your fault, Edren. None of it.

entries.speaker: edren
lines: I'm sorry.

entries.speaker: cael
lines: I know.

entries.speaker: cael
lines:
Torren. Keep the spirits talking. They remember more
than anyone gives them credit for.

entries.speaker: cael
lines:
Sable. You showed up. Every time, you showed up. Don't
stop doing that.

entries.speaker: sable
lines: Yeah, well. Someone has to.

entries.speaker: cael
lines:
Maren. Write it down. All of it. So the next time the
door cracks, the world doesn't have to learn this lesson again.

entries.speaker: maren
lines: I will.

entries.speaker: cael
lines:
Build something. Not for me. Something that works
with the ley lines instead of draining them. You always knew
the craft could be better. Make it better.

entries.speaker: lira
lines: Go.
